# Hemp Decorticator Validation Pack

The validation pack is the **regression suite** for all
future vision work (Phase 17.4, spec §12). It consists
of 6 A3-sized fabrication drawings (one per subsystem
of a hemp decorticator) plus sidecar files that pin
the expected MachineGraph and the minimum composite
score for each.

## Origin

**Synthetic.** Generated by
`tests/fixtures/build_synthetic_fixtures.py`. Per
spec §12.4, the v1 pack is synthetic; real client-
supplied drawings may be added in v1.1+.

## Author

Platform team (auto-generated by the build script).
The script regenerates the pack deterministically
from in-code data; no manual intervention is
required to rebuild the PDFs and sidecar graphs.

## Layout

```
tests/fixtures/drawings/
  hopper_a3.pdf
  conveyor_a3.pdf
  compression_rollers_a3.pdf
  drum_a3.pdf
  spindle_a3.pdf
  frame_a3.pdf
  expected/
    hopper_a3.graph.json
    hopper_a3.score.txt
    conveyor_a3.graph.json
    conveyor_a3.score.txt
    compression_rollers_a3.graph.json
    compression_rollers_a3.score.txt
    drum_a3.graph.json
    drum_a3.score.txt
    spindle_a3.graph.json
    spindle_a3.score.txt
    frame_a3.graph.json
    frame_a3.score.txt
  README.md   (this file)
```

## The 6 subsystems

| Subsystem | Filename | Drawing # | Material | Notes |
|-----------|----------|-----------|----------|-------|
| Hopper | `hopper_a3.pdf` | DWG-HOP-001 | Mild steel | 45 kg, top width 500, bottom width 150 |
| Conveyor | `conveyor_a3.pdf` | DWG-CON-002 | Mild steel | 60 kg, belt length 2000 |
| Compression rollers | `compression_rollers_a3.pdf` | DWG-CMP-003 | Stainless 316 | 2 × Ø240 × 700 |
| Drum | `drum_a3.pdf` | DWG-DRM-004 | EN24T | 380 kg, Ø1500 × 2200 |
| Spindle | `spindle_a3.pdf` | DWG-SPN-005 | EN24T | 95 kg, Ø100 shaft, Ø280 flight |
| Frame | `frame_a3.pdf` | DWG-FRM-006 | Mild steel | 220 kg, rail length 3500 |

## Sidecar formats

### `expected/<name>.graph.json`

The canonical MachineGraph the platform must produce
for this fixture. Single-node graph (one subsystem
per fixture). The regression test
(`tests/test_hemp_decorticator_validation_pack.py`)
asserts the platform's actual graph is a **superset**
of these `nodes` keys (over-extraction allowed;
under-extraction fails per spec §5.1).

Format: the dict shape produced by
`SubsystemNode.to_dict()` in
`app/graph/models.py`. JSON, indented for diff
readability.

### `expected/<name>.score.txt`

A single floating-point number on the first line, the
minimum composite score for a passing run. The
regression test reads the produced revision's
`evaluation.json` `composite` field and asserts it
is `>=` this value.

The initial value is the literal string `TBD`. The
maintainer must baseline it by running the manual
reference config through the orchestrator and
subtracting 0.10 from the resulting composite score
per spec §5.1. See
`docs/VALIDATION_PACK_METHODOLOGY.md` for the
baselining protocol.

## Re-baselining

When the orchestrator is updated in a way that
affects the `composite` score, the maintainer must
re-baseline the sidecar values:

1. Run `python tests/fixtures/build_synthetic_fixtures.py`
   to regenerate the PDFs and graph sidecars (idempotent).
2. For each of the 6 subsystems, run the manual
   reference config through the orchestrator and
   record the composite score.
3. Subtract 0.10 per spec §5.1 to get the new
   threshold.
4. Update the corresponding
   `expected/<name>.score.txt` file.
5. Commit the changes with a message of the form
   "Phase 17.4: Re-baseline validation pack after
   <reason>." Each re-baseline is an amendment to
   the spec per spec §10.

## When the test is skipped

The regression test
(`tests/test_hemp_decorticator_validation_pack.py`)
skips any fixture whose `score.txt` contains `TBD`.
The skip message names the maintainer action
required:

> expected/<name>_a3.score.txt is TBD. The maintainer
> must run the manual reference config for <name>
> through the orchestrator, record the composite
> score, and write (score - 0.10) to the sidecar.

This is the graceful-degradation contract: the
test runs in CI but never fails until the
maintainer has baselined at least one fixture.
